questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass 2 kg is executing SHM and it s potential energy at extreme is 10 J . Velocity at mean position is $\sqrt{8} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. If potential energy at $\mathrm{A} / 2$ (where A is amplitude) is $\mathrm{U}_0$ then find the value of $2 \mathrm{U}_0$ $($ in $J)$.
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{2} k A^2=U_{\max }-U_{\min }=\frac{1}{2} m v_{\max }^2 \Rightarrow U_{\min }=2 J$
Potential energy at $\frac{A}{2}$ is $=U_{\min }+\frac{1}{2} k\left(\frac{A}{2}\right)^2=2+\frac{8}{4}=4 J$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two blocks of masses $m_1=1 \mathrm{~kg}$ and $m_2=2 \mathrm{~kg}$ are connected by a spring of constant $\mathrm{k}=100 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$ and placed over a plank as shown. The coefficient of friction between $\mathrm{m}_2$ and plank is 0.5 and there is no friction between $\mathrm{m}_1$ and plank. Initially the whole system, is at rest. The plank is now pulled to right with constant acceleration $\mathrm{a}=2$ $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Find the amplitude (in cm ) of oscillation of block $\mathrm{m}_1$ relative to plank.
questions.solution:
viewed from frame of plank
[initial condition]
[at the time of maximum compression] $\because 2 m_1 a+m_2 a=8 N$ and $f_{\max }=\mu N=10 N \Rightarrow$ Friction is static $\mathrm{kA}=\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{a} \Rightarrow A=\frac{m_1 a}{k}=2 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A metal rod of length 1.25 m and mass ' m ' is pivoted at one end. A solid sphere of same mass and radius 0.25 m is attached at its center to the free end of the rod and the sphere is free to rotate about its center. The roddisc system performs SHM in vertical plane after being released from the same displaced position. Angular frequency of small oscillations is $n$ $\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$. Find the value of $\mathrm{n}^2$.
questions.solution: $\tau=\left(m g \frac{\ell}{2}+m g \ell\right) \sin \theta ; I=\frac{m \ell^2}{3}+m \ell^2 \therefore \alpha=\frac{\tau}{I}=\frac{3 m g \ell \theta}{2\left[\frac{4 m \ell^2}{3}\right]}=\frac{9}{8} \frac{g \theta}{\ell} ; \omega^2=\frac{9 g}{8 \ell}=9$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of charge $q$ and mass $m$ is suspended from a point on the wall by a rigid massless rod of length $\mathrm{a}=3 \mathrm{~m}$ as shown. Above the point of suspension we have fixed a particle of charge $-q$ at a distance a. On slight displacement from the mean position, the suspended particle executes SHM. Find the time period of $S H M$ is second. [Take $\left.\frac{\mathrm{kq}^2}{2\left(\mathrm{a}^2\right)}=\mathrm{mg}, \mathrm{g}=\pi^2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A spring of force constant $\mathrm{k}=300 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$ connects two blocks having masses 2 kg and 3 kg , lying on a smooth horizontal plane. If the spring block system is released from a stretched position, the number of complete oscillations in 1 minute is $6 n$. Find the value of $n$. Take $\pi=\sqrt{10}$.
questions.solution: The total momentum of the system in the horizontal direction is conserved. We draw the F.B.D., assuming the displacement of the block to be $\mathrm{x}_1$ and $\mathrm{x}_2$ in opposite directions, and the total extension x is given by, $x=x_1+x_2$ and $m_1 x_1=m_2 x_2$ $\therefore \mathrm{m}_1 \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{x}_1}{\mathrm{dt}^2}=-\mathrm{k}\left(\mathrm{x}_1+\mathrm{x}_2\right)$ and $\quad m_2 \frac{d^2 x_2}{d t^2}=-k\left(x_1+x_2\right)$ after suitably manipulating the equations, we get, $\frac{d^2 x_2}{d t^2}=-\frac{-k\left(m_1+m_2\right)}{m_1 m_2} \cdot x$ i.e. the frequency $=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{k\left(m_1+m_2\right)}{m_1 m_2}}$ $=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{300(2+3)}{2 \times 3}}$ $=2.5 \mathrm{~Hz}$ $\therefore \quad$ number of complete oscillations in 1 minute $=\frac{60}{2.5}=24$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small block of mass $m=0.5 \mathrm{~kg}$ is attached to two springs each of force constant $\mathrm{k}=10 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$ as shown in figure. The block is executive SHM with amplitude $\mathrm{A}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \mathrm{~m}$. When the block is at equilibrium position one of the spring breaks without changing momentum of block. New amplitude of oscillation is $\frac{e}{16} \mathrm{~m}$. Find the value of e ?
questions.solution:
$\omega=\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}=\sqrt{40}$ when spring breaks new $\omega=\sqrt{20}$
Equilibrium position of original system (2k) $x_0=m g$ or $x_0=\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m}$
New equilibrium is at $\mathrm{kx}=\mathrm{mg} ; \mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}$ thus $\mathrm{v}_{\max }=\mathrm{A} \omega=(\sqrt{40}) \times\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)$
$\frac{\sqrt{10}}{2}=\sqrt{20}\left[A^2-\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^2\right]^{1 / 2} ; \frac{10}{4}=20\left[A^2-\frac{1}{16}\right] ; \frac{1}{8}+A^2=A^{\prime 2} ; A^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2} n=50 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: There is an isolated planet having mass $2 M$ and radius $2 R$, where $M$ and $R$ are the mass and radius of the earth. A simple pendulum having mass $m$ and length $2 R$ is made to small oscillations on the planet. The time period of SHM of pendulum is $(0.7 \mathrm{~K}+0.5)$ seconds. Find the value of K. (take $\pi=3.00, \mathrm{~g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2, \sqrt{2}=1.41$ )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & g=\frac{G M}{R^2} \\ & g^{\prime}=\frac{G \cdot 2 m}{4 R^2} \\ & g^{\prime}=\frac{g}{2} \\ & F=-m g^{\prime} \sin \propto \\ & F=-\frac{m g}{2} \cdot \sin 2 \theta \end{aligned}$ As $\theta$ is much small $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{ma}=-\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2} \cdot 2 \theta \\ & \mathrm{a}=-\mathrm{g} \cdot \theta \\ & \mathrm{a}=-\mathrm{g} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{x}}{2 \mathrm{R}} \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \omega & =\sqrt{\frac{g}{2 R}} f \\ \mathrm{~T} & =2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{2 R}{g}} \\ \mathrm{~T} & =2 \times 3.14 \sqrt{\frac{2 \times 6400 \times 10^3}{10}} \\ = & 2 \times 3 \times 800 \times 1.41 \\ \mathrm{~T} & =6768 \mathrm{~s} .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A cyclist turns her bicycle upside down to tinker with it. After she gets it upside down, notices the front wheel executing a small-amplitude, back-and-forth rotational motion with a period of 12 s . Considering the wheel to be a thin ring of total mass 800 g and radius 20 cm , whose only irregularity is the presence of the small tyre valve stem, determine the mass of the valve stem (in gm).
[Take : $\pi^2=\mathrm{g}$ and appropriate approximation]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
